ddls:
CREATE TABLE "debug_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "bit_number" INTEGER /* Bit number representing the status flag */,
  "field_name" TEXT /* Name of the status field */,
  "init_value" INTEGER /* Initial value of the status flag */,
  "current_value" INTEGER /* Current value of the status flag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status flag functionality */,
  "hardware_component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hardware component associated with this status flag */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to this status flag */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the status flag is critical */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "firmware_versions" (
  "firmware_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each firmware version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the firmware */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the firmware version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the firmware features or changes */,
  "hardware_component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hardware component associated with this firmware version */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* Changelog of changes in the firmware version */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the firmware version is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("firmware_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "debug_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each debugging session */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer conducting the session */,
  "firmware_id" INTEGER /* ID of the firmware version tested */,
  "hardware_component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hardware component being debugged */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status flag being monitored */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the debugging session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the debugging session */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or findings from the session */,
  "session_type" TEXT /* Type of debugging session (e.g., initial setup, bug fixing, optimization) */,
  "session_status" TEXT /* Status of the session (e.g., started, paused, completed, aborted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_debug_sessions_hardware_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("hardware_component_id") REFERENCES firmware_versions ("hardware_component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_debug_sessions_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES engineers ("engineer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_debug_sessions_firmware_id FOREIGN KEY ("firmware_id") REFERENCES firmware_versions ("firmware_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_debug_sessions_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES debug_status ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engineers" (
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engineer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the engineer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the engineer */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the engineer (e.g., developer, tester) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the engineer belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the engineer for quick contact */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the engineer is currently active in the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engineer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the debugging session associated with this log */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the log entry was created */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message or detail of the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log entry (e.g., error, warning, info) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., critical, high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES debug_sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_control" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for access control entry */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer for whom the access is set */,
  "feature" TEXT /* Feature or function of the debugging platform */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Level of permission (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the engineer belongs to */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access control entry is currently active */,
  "feature_description" TEXT /* Description of the feature */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES engineers ("engineer_id")
)
